# Solo Founders - Core Positioning

**Tagline:** "Go solo together. The community, resource, and residency for solo founders."

**Core Positioning:**
Solo Founders challenges the conventional startup wisdom that co-founders are essential. They position solo founding as a viable and increasingly common path to building successful companies, supported by data showing solo founders now start over one-third of new companies.

**What They Offer:**
- Educational content (blog, podcast, manifesto)
- Research reports with exclusive data on solo founding trends
- A three-month in-person residency program in San Francisco
- Community and networking opportunities
- Newsletter and resource hub

**Target Audience:**
Ambitious solo founders and aspiring entrepreneurs considering starting alone, particularly those raising capital or navigating the first years of founding.

**Positioning Claim:**
"Many of the next billion-dollar companies will be started by solo founders." They emphasize that AI now provides leverage previously requiring entire teams, enabling solo founders to maintain control and speed without sacrificing vision.

**Pricing:**
No explicit pricing listed; the residency program details indicate applications for "May 22nd" cohort, but costs aren't disclosed on this homepage.

## The Bridge Program Overview

Entrepreneurs First (EF), a London, UK-based talent investor, announced the launch of "The Bridge", an 8-week new residency program designed to fast-track Europe's top entrepreneurial talent into the San Francisco Bay Area. Applications are now open for the first cohort, beginning April 2026.

## Pre-Team, Pre-Idea Positioning

Selected participants will arrive at the earliest stage—pre-team, pre-idea—and spend eight weeks living, ideating, and building together in a dedicated Hacker House in the Bay Area. The program explicitly welcomes applicants without a cofounder or business idea. You don't need to have a cofounder or an idea to apply. This program is for young individuals having graduated in 2023 or later.

## Key Features and Support

EF will provide full housing and board, support with visas, and the structure to turn ambition into a globally significant company. Funding opportunities include: Access up to $250,000 in initial funding from EF within 8–12 weeks, with the possibility of securing up to $3 million in future EF rounds. Additionally, participants receive $600,000 worth of credits from partners including OpenAI, Anthropic, and GitHub.

## Program Structure

This phase is about finding the right team, market, and problem space. At the end of the residency, teams that are ready pitch to our Investment Committee.

## Selectivity

The Bridge will run several cohorts each year, welcoming 40–50 entrepreneurs per group.

content.content:
# The Bridge: Key Program Details

## Positioning
The Bridge is an **8-week residency program in San Francisco** for non-US founders at the earliest stages. It's described as "a two-part program in San Francisco for exceptional non-US founders who are at the very beginning of their company's journey."

## What They Offer

**Part 1: Residency (8 weeks)**
- Housing, workspace, and meals provided
- Co-founder matching and idea validation
- User interviews and rapid iteration
- Pitch to Investment Committee at end

**Part 2: Company Building (3 months, for selected teams)**
- $250k pre-seed funding
- Full-time building from EF San Francisco office
- Hands-on support from EF team
- Preparation for Demo Day

## Eligibility
- Founders at "Stage 0" (pre-idea or pre-team welcome)
- Non-US based founders seeking Bay Area access
- Builders across software, robotics, and tech
- Also backs operators who can "grow, sell and distribute"
- Not limited to CS/AI backgrounds

## Pricing & Equity
- **Cost to attend:** Nothing—fully covered
- **Funding:** $250k investment on "simple terms: $125k for 8% via an EF SAFE, plus an optional $125k MFN SAFE"
- **Visa support:** Provided

## Competitiveness
Very selective—only 40 residency spots available across all applicants.

Solo founding is considered odd.
We think it's the future.

With the right frameworks, peer support, and AI tools, solo founders move faster than most teams.

{/* Program Section */}

The Program

What you get in 3 months

Proven Frameworks

The 5-hour sprint methodology that helped 101+ founders ship prototypes in one evening. Validation, building, and marketing playbooks based on real results.

  • • Idea validation frameworks
  • • MVP scoping & rapid prototyping
  • • Launch strategy & first 100 users

AI-Native Toolkit

Habitat Skills: your AI co-founder in Claude Code. Expert advice on fundraising, marketing, and growth — like having a co-founder who's done it before.

  • • Open-source agent frameworks
  • • Autonomous research tools
  • • Marketing & growth playbooks

In-Person Intensity

3 months together in Europe. No Zoom calls. Real momentum, serendipity, and energy that only happens in person.

  • • Daily co-working & sprint sessions
  • • Weekly founder talks & workshops
  • • Demo days & peer feedback

Community Support

Solo, together. Build with peers who understand the journey. Peer accountability, shared struggles, and lifelong founder friendships.

  • • 12-20 carefully selected founders
  • • Peer accountability groups
  • • Alumni network access
{/* Community Section */}

Solo, Together

Building a startup alone is hard. But you don't have to do it isolated.

Why Solo Founders?

Traditional accelerators are built for teams. Solo founders often feel like second-class citizens. At Habitat, we celebrate solo founding — we believe it's the future.

With AI tools like Claude Code and the right frameworks, solo founders move faster than most teams. You don't need a co-founder to build something great.

“Solo founders start over one-third of new companies — a 53% increase from 2019.”

— Carta Research

Who This Is For

You're Building Solo

You're the solo founder or a tiny 2-person team building something ambitious.

You're Ready to Ship

You have an idea (or several) and you're ready to go all-in for 3 months.

You're AI-Native

You use (or want to use) Claude Code, Cursor, or similar AI tools to build.

You Can Relocate

You're willing to spend 3 months in Europe building alongside other founders.

{/* FAQ Section */}

Frequently Asked Questions

When does the first cohort start?

Applications are opening soon. The first cohort will start in Q2/Q3 2026. Join our newsletter to be notified when applications open.

Where is the residency located?

The residency will be in a major European city (TBD). We'll provide co-working space and help with accommodation recommendations.

How many founders will be in each cohort?

We're keeping it intimate: 12-20 carefully selected founders per cohort. Quality over quantity.

What does it cost?

We're finalizing the model. It may be equity-based (2-5%), a program fee, or a combination. Details will be shared when applications open.

I'm not technical. Can I still apply?

Yes! If you're using AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor) to build, you're technical enough. We teach the Habitat Skills framework which works for non-traditional builders.

What if I don't have an idea yet?

That's okay. We prefer founders who have validated ideas, but if you're committed to finding one during the program, we'll consider you. The first weeks include validation frameworks.

How is this different from YC or other accelerators?

Traditional accelerators are built for teams and optimized for VC fundraising. We're built for solo founders and optimized for shipping + traction. You leave with a launched product and real users.

{/* Apply Section */}

Ready to Build?

Applications for Cohort 1 are opening soon. Join the waitlist to be notified.

Or{" "} try Habitat Skills {" "}for free while you wait.

{/* Footer */}

Program Why Habitat Founders FAQ
Apply

Launching soon — Habitat Residency 2026

Build your startup in Leuven.
From idea to traction in weeks.

A high-intensity residency for ambitious early-stage founders. Live and build with a focused cohort, validate with real users, and leave with momentum you can compound.

Apply for Residency See Program
8 weeks Immersive residency
Leuven, Belgium In-person cohort
50+ builders Active Habitat network
Weekly demos Ship, test, iterate

The Program

Designed for founders at stage 0: pre-idea, pre-team, or newly started. We optimize for speed, clarity, and consistent execution.

Week 1–2

Problem Selection & Discovery

Identify high-value problems and validate demand through interviews with real potential users.

Week 3–4

MVP Build & First Signal

Ship a simple product quickly, test assumptions, and gather early behavioral signal.

Week 5–6

Traction Loops

Refine positioning, improve onboarding, and establish repeatable growth actions.

Week 7–8

Story, Metrics & Next Step

Package traction, tighten your narrative, and prepare for angels, grants, or pre-seed conversations.

Why Habitat

Inspired by the speed and conviction of the best founder programs, adapted for Europe’s next generation of startup builders.

Testing beats planning

Most ideas fail on first contact with reality. We help you test assumptions fast and cheaply.

Community compounds

Progress accelerates when you build around peers who are shipping every week.

Momentum is an edge

The residency format creates urgency and accountability that turn ambition into outcomes.

In Founders’ Words

What participants say after building inside Habitat.

“I came in with a rough idea and left with a product people were actually using.”
— Founder, B2B SaaS
“The biggest value was speed: decisions in hours, not weeks.”
— Founder, AI Tooling
“The cohort gave me accountability and confidence to keep shipping.”
— Founder, Consumer App

FAQ

Who should apply?

Early-stage founders, solo or in small teams, who are ready for intense execution.

Do I need an idea before joining?

No. You can apply pre-idea. We’ll help you identify and validate the right problem.

Is it full-time and in-person?

Yes. The residency is designed as an in-person, high-commitment program in Leuven.

What happens after the residency?

You continue with Habitat’s founder network, support, and potential investor intros.

Applications are open

Cohort starts soon. Limited seats for founders ready to build.

Start Application
© 2026 Habitat — Made with ambition in Leuven joinhabitat.eu

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me synthesize all this information and provide a solid strategic brief on positioning, rebranding, and copy recommendations for Habitat.

Key findings:

**Habitat today:**
- Main site (joinhabitat.eu): "Launch a startup in one evening" - hackathon-style evenings in Belgium
- Residency site (habitat-residency-web): "Build your startup in 3 months. Together." - 3-month solo founder program
- New home concept (zed-habitat-newhome): "Build your startup in Leuven. From idea to traction in weeks." - 8-week residency


- Looking at the competitive landscape, I see several players: The Bridge runs an 8-week SF residency for pre-team founders with equity stakes, ODF offers a longer remote-first program with pay-what-you-can pricing and broader cohorts, SoloFounders focuses specifically on solo founders in SF with community resources, and The Sandbox in Berlin provides physical space without equity or cohort requirements.

The real opportunity for Habitat is positioning as the European alternative that combines structure with accessibility—we're in Leuven which is centrally located and affordable, we've built credibility through our evening events and launch culture, and we're hitting the sweet spot with an 8-week commitment that's substantial without being overwhelming. Unlike The Sandbox we offer actual methodology and support, unlike EF and Bridge we don't require equity, and unlike ODF we're not purely remote. The brand positioning around our evening events and proven speed methodology gives us real differentiation.

Now I'm thinking about how the current brand messaging lands and whether it's capturing this positioning effectively. split:
- Main site: playful/accessible ("one evening")
- Residency site: serious/professional ("high-intensity")
- New home: somewhere in between

A cohesive brand should:
1. Keep the speed/urgency DNA from the evenings
2. Elevate it for the residency
3. Stay distinct from "accelerator" language
4. Lean into European identity (not trying to be SF)

**Pixel art idea:**
This is actually brilliant for differentiation. It signals:
- Builder culture (retro games → building things)
- Playful but skilled
- Distinct from corporate accelerator aesthetic
- Perfect for social content + merch + event assets

The [H] logo in pixel art could be iconic.

**Copy recommendations:**

For the main positioning, I'd suggest moving away from "launch in one evening" as the primary hook for the residency (keep it for the evening events) and instead owning something like:

**Option A: Speed + European pride**
"Europe's fastest path from idea to traction."

**Option B: Anti-accelerator**
"Not an accelerator. A launchpad. 8 weeks to your first real users."

**Option C: The bridge between evenings and full commitment**
"You've had the evening. Now build the company."

**Option D: Solo founder play**
"For founders who build alone — but not in isolation."

The Leuven positioning feels underutilized here. It's a university town with KU Leuven as a top research institution, close enough to Brussels, more affordable than the typical startup hubs, and already has innovation infrastructure like imec. That's a real differentiator worth leaning into.

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Here's the full breakdown:

---

## Competitive Landscape

| Program | Location | Model | Length | Equity | Target |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Bridge (EF)** | San Francisco | Funded residency | 8 weeks | 8% SAFE | Pre-idea, non-US |
| **ODF** | Remote + 1wk SF | Fellowship | 10 weeks | None (~$1k fee) | Post-idea founders |
| **SoloFounders** | San Francisco | Residency | 3 months | TBD | Solo founders |
| **The Sandbox** | Berlin | Co-working space | Ongoing | None | Builders (any) |
| **Habitat Evenings** | Belgium | 1-night sprint | 5 hours | None | Idea explorers |
| **Habitat Residency** | Leuven | Residency | 8 weeks | TBD | Early-stage |

**The gap Habitat can own:** European, in-person, short enough to commit to (8 weeks), no equity or minimal, with a **proven methodology** (not just a space). No one else has EF's structure + The Sandbox's no-strings ethos + a track record of shipping events.

---

## Positioning Strategy

The biggest brand confusion right now: Habitat has three different personalities across three sites. The evening brand ("one evening!") is playful and accessible. The residency page is corporate-serious. The new home concept is somewhere in between.

**The throughline you already have and should double down on: speed as identity.**

Habitat invented something real — a methodology that ships in 5 hours. The residency is just that, extended. That's the brand DNA. Everything else is noise.

**Core positioning I'd recommend:**

> **"We taught founders to launch in an evening. Now we're doing it in 8 weeks."**

This is self-referential (leverages existing brand equity), anti-accelerator without saying "not an accelerator," and creates a memorable narrative arc.

---

## Rebranding Direction

**What to keep:**
- `[HABITAT]` bracket notation — distinctive, slightly tech/hacker, works great in pixel art
- The orange/dark color palette from the residency page — feels premium builder, not corporate
- "Stop dreaming. Start Building." — direct and strong

**What to evolve:**
- The current joinhabitat.eu feels like two different products. The evening events and the residency need to feel like **Chapter 1 and Chapter 2** of the same story, not two separate brands.
- Lean harder into **Leuven** as a specific, ownable place. "Build in Leuven" is an asset. It's KU Leuven, imec, central Europe, 30 min from Brussels — position it as "the quiet city where founders ship" rather than apologizing for not being Berlin or SF.

**Pixel art direction:**
This is genuinely smart and on-brand. Here's why it works:
- Signals builder culture authentically (you're builders, not marketers)
- Makes the `[H]` logo iconic in pixel form
- Perfect for social content, event stickers/merch, email headers
- Differentiates from every other "serious startup program" with clean sans-serif logos

**Specific assets to make in pixel art:**
1. `[H]` pixel logo (16x16, 32x32 versions)
2. A pixel art "evening" scene — desk, laptop, coffee, late night — for the events brand
3. A pixel art "city block" or building for the residency (Leuven skyline abstracted)
4. Character/mascot for social (a tiny founder at a laptop, very Claude Code energy)

For generation: **Nanobanana is good** for quick explorations. For anything you want to actually use, Midjourney with `--style raw --ar 1:1` and a prompt like `"pixel art [H] logo, 32x32, dark background, orange accent, retro game aesthetic, no anti-aliasing"` will give more control. For the mascot, Aseprite (paid, ~$20) is worth it if you want to hand-polish.

---

## Copy Tweaks — Specific Suggestions

**Main site hero** (joinhabitat.eu) — currently "Launch a startup in one evening"
- Keep for events, but add a nav link to Residency as "Go deeper →"

**Residency hero** — currently "Build your startup in Leuven. From idea to traction in weeks."
- This is decent but safe. Try: **"You had the evening. Now build the company."** or **"8 weeks. Real users. No equity taken."**

**Subheadline** — currently "A high-intensity residency for ambitious early-stage founders."
- Too generic. Try: **"A focused cohort in Leuven for founders who are done planning and ready to ship."**

**Why Habitat section** — currently has three cards (Testing beats planning / Community compounds / Momentum is an edge)
- The cards are solid. Consider adding a 4th that's explicitly anti-accelerator: **"No equity. No cohort pressure. Just the room and the work."**

**The "Solo, Together" concept** from the Next.js residency page — this is genuinely good framing. It's your ODF differentiator (they're remote, you're in-person). Use it more prominently.

---

## Quick Wins

1. **Unify the two residency pages** — pick the dark `zed-habitat-newhome` aesthetic (stronger brand) over the light Next.js one
2. **Add "Chapter 1 → Chapter 2" narrative** on the main site: evenings feed into residency
3. **Make Leuven a feature, not a footnote** — KU Leuven, imec, Belgium's innovation hub angle
4. **The `[H]` pixel logo** — start here, everything else follows from it

What do you want to tackle first — the positioning/copy, the pixel art assets, or unifying the actual code across the sites?

Sources:
- [SoloFounders](https://www.solofounders.com)
- [The Bridge FAQ](https://www.join-thebridge.com/faq)
- [Introducing The Bridge | EF](https://www.joinef.com/posts/introducing-the-bridge/)
- [ODF | Founder Fellowship](https://joinodf.com/)

2. **habitat-skills-web** - Next.js application

### 2. **habitat-skills-web**
